Annual · Free Entry · Open to All

Probasi Cultural Festival

An evening celebration of Bengali and Eastern Indian performing arts — music, dance, drama and community — open to all of Tauranga.

Next festival — 17 & 18 October 2026
Our flagship event

An evening that belongs to everyone

The Probasi Cultural Festival is TPCH's annual celebration of Bengali and Eastern Indian performing arts. Held each year alongside Durga Puja in October, it brings together community performers — musicians, dancers, poets and artists — for an evening of culture, colour and shared joy.

Entry is free and all are welcome. The festival is our gift to Tauranga — a window into the richness of our heritage, and an invitation to celebrate with us.

Now in its third year, the Probasi Cultural Festival has grown from a small community gathering into a cherished annual event, drawing audiences from across the Bay of Plenty.

What to expect

A night of culture & community

Each festival features a full evening programme of live performances by community artists.

Music & Song

Rabindra Sangeet, Nazrul Geeti, folk and contemporary Bengali music performed live — voices and instruments carrying the soul of Bengal to Tauranga.

Dance

Classical and folk dance traditions of Bengal and Eastern India — from graceful Bharatanatyam to energetic folk performances — brought to life on stage.

Drama & Recitation

Theatrical presentations, poetry recitation and dramatic performances — celebrating the literary and storytelling traditions of our culture.

Children's performances

Our youngest community members take the stage — singing, dancing and reciting, showing that the next generation carries our culture forward with pride.

Community spirit

The festival closes with a community meal open to all attendees — sharing food, conversation and the warmth of a community that is more than the sum of its parts.

Free entry

The Probasi Cultural Festival is free for everyone. No tickets, no barriers — just an open invitation to come, enjoy and be part of our community celebration.

Coming up

Probasi Cultural Festival 2026

17 & 18 October 2026

Planning is already underway for our fourth annual festival. Venue and programme details will be announced closer to the event.

Free entry Open to all Tauranga, Bay of Plenty Venue to be announced

From our stage

Past festivals

2025
Featured production

Shyama

Tagore Dance Drama

A Rabindra Nritya Natya brought to the stage for Festival 2025 — Shyama tells the story of a courtesan whose love for a falsely accused merchant becomes a meditation on guilt, justice and sacrifice, told entirely through Tagore's music and movement.

Produced & directed by Kutu Mukherjee · Choreography by Goldy Dey-Bhowmik

Get involved

Perform at the festival

Are you a musician, dancer, poet, actor or performer? The Probasi Cultural Festival welcomes community artists of all experience levels. If you'd like to perform at our 2026 festival, we'd love to hear from you.

Tauranga Probasi
Cultural Heritage Inc.

A registered community organisation preserving Bengali & Eastern Indian cultural heritage in Tauranga, Aotearoa New Zealand.
